center.name: Gunung Ambang Nature Reserve
center.summary: Gunung Ambang Nature Reserve是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联2种鸟类，包括Stephan's emerald dove、Scaly-breasted kingfisher，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: regalis is found in the southeast, but is now regarded as its own species, the plain-backed kingfisher. In the Minahassa Peninsula of northern Sulawesi, the nominate subspecies is found in the Gunung Ambang Nature Reserve in primary forest at elevations between above sea level. Its habitat is the dense understorey of undisturbed primary forest

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: In places where the Stephan's emerald dove does not occur alongside the common emerald dove, it inhabits a much more diverse array of woodland habitats, such as scrubby coconut plantations and coastal forests. In New Guinea, where Stephan's emerald dove lives alongside the white-breasted ground dove, it restricts itself to forest edge and secondary forests, with the ground dove occurring in the interior of forests. Stephan's emerald dove dislikes drier forests, especially in the dry season. On Sulawesi, it is known from humid evergreen forests and secondary coastal forests. It usually occurs below , but has been recorded at elevations of in Gunung Ambang Nature Reserve. It is known to inhabit heavily degraded and logged forests on Taliabu. In New Guinea, it is known from forest edge and secondary forests in the lowlands and is much more common in forest fragments than continuous forest. It is usually found below and is most common near sea level, but has been found as high as in the Kumawa Mountains and on Mount Karimui. On the Admiralty Islands, it is much more common in degraded habitats and coconut plantations than primary forest, and occurs at elevations of up to . Stephan's emerald dove migrates locally in response to local climactic conditions and food availability. A carcass of the dove was found on the Carstenz Massif in New Guinea in the 1970s at an elevation of . This is almost above the normal altitudes the dove inhabits, suggesting it may also wander more widely
